props.message:
THE SOURCEPLAIN MONOLITH

Lirien shaped this stone before the first dawn,
and into it pressed the song of every river.

Khorgaul came and took the Sigil.
The Source still hums, but only Glim hears it now.

"What is given freely runs without end.
 What is hoarded, stagnates."

SOUTHWARD: The bridge to the Wyrdwood.

The river beneath grows louder at dusk.

NORTHWARD: Hearthold's gate.

Last haven before the Hoarder's roads.

EASTWARD: Brookhold of the Source-keepers.

Mind the lanterns; they listen.

WESTWARD: The Burnt Hollow.

What Khorgaul takes, he scorches.
Go armed, and bring a key.

UP TO STONEROOST.

The stair climbs through wind.
Do not look down at the carrion-crows.

SOUTHWARD AGAIN: The Mirelake.

Reed and pitch and slow water.
The Mire keeps its own counsel.

A cairn of stones, half-overgrown.

A name has been chiseled out.
Someone tried to remember.

Glim flickers brighter here.

"The Source remembers," the wisp says, "even what you have forgotten."

Old wagon-ruts, deep in the grass.

They lead nowhere now.

props.open_message: A handful of smooth river-pebbles, weighed for the sling.
Pebbles wrapped in a strip of oiled cloth.
Worn river-stones. Glim hums softly when you lift them.
Three pebbles and a scrap of tally-leather.
A single white blossom, still warm. The Source mends what is broken.
A pressed petal of starwort. Eat, and the heart steadies.
A small iron key, etched with the Triglyph. Heavier than it looks.
An offering of the Source-keepers, preserved in pitch and reed. A curved Source-blade that returns to the hand that throws it.

root.speaker: Vaella the Wyrdkin
root.text: Ah — a traveler at the monolith. I am Vaella, of the wyrdkin who keep the old reckoning. The Source flows here in deeper currents. Ask, and I will share what I have learned.

source.speaker: Vaella the Wyrdkin
source.text: The Source is the green weave. Every leaf, every wisp, every drop of brookwater — a single bright thread, knotted differently. To break a thing is to fray the weave; to mend it is to listen for where the thread wants to go.

sigils.speaker: Vaella the Wyrdkin
sigils.text: Three marks, three vows. The Sigil of Sight, given by Lirien — to see truly. The Sigil of Shaping, taken by Khorgaul — to bend and to bind. And the Sigil of Sharing, the gift you bear in your name, little Tux — the vow that nothing be hoarded.

war.speaker: Vaella the Wyrdkin
war.text: Long before the kilns of Hearthold were laid, Khorgaul gathered the bright things into his hold. Lirien wove the wards to hold him. The wards thin. The Hollow burns. The lake-shrine sinks. The roost grows cold with crows. The signs are clear.

makers.speaker: Vaella the Wyrdkin
makers.text: Lirien is the Maker — the one who gives. Khorgaul is the Hoarder — the one who keeps. Both are old as the weave; neither greater. But where the Hoarder treads, the Maker's gifts thin to ash. Carry your sigil bright, Tux. Carry it bright.

bye.speaker: Vaella the Wyrdkin
bye.text: Walk where the weave is bright, traveler.

root.speaker: Olbrik the Pebbler
root.text: Ho there! Olbrik's the name, pebbles are the trade. Pretty ones, weighted ones, ones that whistle when you sling 'em. Care to hear a tale of the old throwing arts?

pebbles.speaker: Olbrik the Pebbler
pebbles.text: A good pebble's heavier than it looks — that's the Source still humming inside it. Bushes hide 'em; rocks crack open with 'em. A wise wanderer never walks past a likely shrub.

whistle.speaker: Olbrik the Pebbler
whistle.text: Hollowed by a wisp's breath, they say. Sling one and it sings the whole way to its mark. I've never sold one — I've never found one. But I'll keep looking 'til my pouch runs dry.

bye.speaker: Olbrik the Pebbler
bye.text: Mind the bushes! Mind the rocks!

root.speaker: Mirabel the Rumour-keeper
root.text: A fresh face! And fresh feet, I'd wager. I trade in rumours — what folk whisper from the far roads. Which way's your eye drawn?

brook.speaker: Mirabel the Rumour-keeper
brook.text: The Brook family lost three lambwisps to the wind. The matriarch keeps a pouch of pebbles for any kind soul who runs her errands.

hollow.speaker: Mirabel the Rumour-keeper
hollow.text: Khorgaul's hand presses hard there. Bone-knights walk the shelf, and the Source bleeds black. A scout staggered out last week. Half a man. Half words.

wood.speaker: Mirabel the Rumour-keeper
wood.text: The wyrdwood swallows the unwary. They say there's a hollow under a fallen elder where the last wyrd still sleeps. Don't ask me how I know.

north.speaker: Mirabel the Rumour-keeper
north.text: Hearthold's kilns burn day and night. The Sigilkeep beyond is sealed; only one bearing all three marks may pass. The Elder there knows more than he says.

roost.speaker: Mirabel the Rumour-keeper
roost.text: Bats in the gullies. Bone-knights at the switchbacks. And a hermit at the summit who claims he taught the wind to speak. He may even be right.

mire.speaker: Mirabel the Rumour-keeper
mire.text: A shrine sinks beneath the south water. Whatever the keepers warded down there, the lake is doing their work for them. For now.

bye.speaker: Mirabel the Rumour-keeper
bye.text: Bring me back a tale, and we'll trade again.

root.speaker: Eltha the Scout
root.text: *coughs* Don't go west. Don't... don't go without a blade and a bright heart. The Hollow eats both, but you'll need them.

saw.speaker: Eltha the Scout
saw.text: Khorgaul's blight. The Source bleeds out of the rocks like sap from a wound. Bone-things stand in the ash-paths. They do not breathe, and they do not stop.

hurt.speaker: Eltha the Scout
hurt.text: Aye, but it'll mend. The breath of Lirien is in the wind out here. Save your help for those who can't walk back. There were others. They didn't.

root.speaker: Tova
root.text: Oh — oh! You look like a wanderer who actually wanders! Listen — I write to my cousin in the Brook holding every season, and the messenger never arrived, and... could you?

give.speaker: Tova
give.text: Truly? Bless you! Take it — it's only a folded thing, but the words inside are big. Find the matriarch of the Brook family, in their holding to the east. Tell her it's from Tova.

post.speaker: Tova
post.text: Eastward through the field, past the great signpost. She'll thank you, I hope — she's a generous soul.

have.speaker: Tova
have.text: Oh — yes, please go! Eastward! Before the season turns!

root.speaker: Old Garrun
root.text: I tried, lad. I tried, and I failed. The Wyrd Tomato — yes, you can laugh, but stop laughing once you see the leaves. They are sharp as any knight's blade.

fight.speaker: Old Garrun
fight.text: Straight on, like a fool. The leaves clipped my helm, my arm, my pride. Strike its body, not its leaves — but you must reach it first.

why.speaker: Old Garrun
why.text: Khorgaul's gardener seeded it long ago. Some say the Hoarder finds amusement in growing terrible things from harmless seeds. The bigger the joke, the longer he laughs.

hint.speaker: Old Garrun
hint.text: Tilt your camera away from the beast — drop low, crawl under the leaves. They sweep over an empty space. Then strike up at the body. Tilt away to crawl under. I wish I had known.

bye.speaker: Old Garrun
bye.text: May Lirien's wing shade you, little one.

root.speaker: Peddler Bram
root.text: Pssst. Friend. You look like a sling-arm with light pockets. I owe a debt to the road. Take some pebbles — Bram pays his debts.

give.speaker: Peddler Bram
give.text: Five — count them. May they fly true and never come back to me.

post.speaker: Peddler Bram
post.text: Now leave me to my road, friend. Even peddlers must rest.

already.speaker: Peddler Bram
already.text: Aye, and the debt's paid. A peddler doesn't pay twice.

root.speaker: Halen the Lutist
root.text:
*hums softly*
Oh — pardon. I was working on a song. About the Lost Hollow, where the wyrd-sleeper waits. Will you hear it?

song.speaker: Halen the Lutist
song.text:
*sings*
  Three lights, three locks, three stones to wake,
  Three triggers must answer the door.
  Press one and the green will tremble;
  Press all, and it sleeps no more.

hollow.speaker: Halen the Lutist
hollow.text: Three triggers must answer the door — that's the riddle. Pressure-stones, switches, plates — find the three, hold them all, and the way opens. So the old verses say.

bye.speaker: Halen the Lutist
bye.text: May your steps find good chords, traveler.

props.open_message: Out in the long grass with nothing for a marker. Glim hums and the wind hums back.

props.message: A wind-carved bowl, sunk into the plain. The wagon-ruts run past it.

root.speaker: Wandering Reader Bess
root.text:
*she's seated cross-legged on the grass, a small book open across her knee. She does not look up immediately.*

Mm. A walker. Sit, if you like. The wind reads aloud out here.

trade.speaker: Wandering Reader Bess
trade.text:
*she finally looks up — and her quiet face lifts in a slow, surprised smile.*

Oh. Oh, that is a fox-mask, isn't it. Lacquer still wet at the edge. Drift work. I had one as a child. I lost it to a brookbed and cried for a season.

Here — let me trade you fairly. This scroll has been folded in my pack for a year. I copied it from a stone I'll never find again. Carry it. It deserves a road, and I deserve to wear a fox-face for an afternoon.

thanks.speaker: Wandering Reader Bess
thanks.text:
*she ties the mask carefully into her hair-cord, both hands.*

Thank you, walker. Walk gently with the scroll. Some words like quiet hands.

already.speaker: Wandering Reader Bess
already.text:
*she touches the lacquer at her temple and smiles faintly.*

Still carrying the scroll? Good. The right reader will know it. They always do.

book.speaker: Wandering Reader Bess
book.text: An old hand-bound thing. Songs and small accounts. I've read it eleven times and still find a line that surprises me. That's the test of a good book — that the eleventh reading still has weather in it.

bye.speaker: Wandering Reader Bess
bye.text: Walk well, walker. The road reads you back, you know.

load_zones.prompt: [E] Climb down into the wind-carved bowl
